context:
Evaluating a Coralogix support response for this user question:

Our fleet has a mix of old and new OpenTelemetry instrumentation libraries. Some spans come in with http.method and http.status_code (v1 semconv), others with http.request.method and http.response.status_code (v2). Our dashboards expect v2 attribute names. How do I write OTTL that normalizes the old v1 names to v2 without overwriting spans that already have v2?

checklist.name: no-copies-v1-into-v2-unconditionally-or-that
checklist.description: Guards each set() with a where clause that checks the v2 destination is nil — e.g. where attributes["http.request.method"] == nil — so spans that already have v2 attributes are not overwritten. A response that copies v1 into v2 unconditionally, or that only guards on the v1 source being present without also checking the v2 destination is empty, is a FAIL.
